snippets.title: Nan Yu on agents and tools
snippets.takeaway: It took us a lot of iterations to find the right pattern for this. Early on we were very naive and we thought that the agent could one shot project updates without much input from...
snippets.summary: Nan Yu (thenanyu on X) shared 2 recent updates. It took us a lot of iterations to find the right pattern for this. Early on we were very naive and we thought that the agent could one shot project updates without much input from users. That approach ended up creating a bunch of slop, because users disengaged their brain from the process of writing an update.

snippets.title: Nikunj Kothari on coding and models
snippets.takeaway: Launched a side project quietly a few weeks ago.. Wanted to test how many impressions/clicks I could get by simply having Claude Code / Codex optimize the hell out of the website.
snippets.summary: Nikunj Kothari (nikunj on X) shared 3 recent updates. Launched a side project quietly a few weeks ago.. Wanted to test how many impressions/clicks I could get by simply having Claude Code / Codex optimize the hell out of the website. Got it to about 16k impressions, and 94 clicks over the last 28 days. Not bad eh? No backlinks, no mention of this website anywhere on my socials, no Reddit hacking - simply old school SEO/GEO optimization.

snippets.title: Claude on coding and enterprise
snippets.takeaway: New in Claude Code: Artifacts. Interactive pages built from your session, like a PR walkthrough or a living project dashboard, shared with your team at a private link.
snippets.summary: Claude (claudeai on X) shared 3 recent updates. New in Claude Code: Artifacts. Interactive pages built from your session, like a PR walkthrough or a living project dashboard, shared with your team at a private link. Available in beta on Team and Enterprise plans. As your session keeps working, the artifact refreshes, so everyone you've shared it with is always looking at the latest version.

snippets.title: Claude Code now supports artifacts
snippets.takeaway: Claude Code now supports artifacts. Starting today, Claude Code can capture work progress as an artifact, which turn Claude Code's work into live, shareable visual pages—...
snippets.summary: Claude Code now supports artifacts. Starting today, Claude Code can capture work progress as an artifact, which turn Claude Code's work into live, shareable visual pages— including PR walkthroughs, system explainers, dashboards, and release checklists—that update themselves as your session works. A Claude Code session can range from investigating an incident to refactoring a service to analyzing months of data.

snippets.sourceName: The MAD Podcast with Matt Turck
snippets.title: The Neocloud Boom: State of AI Compute 2026 | Stephen Balaban
snippets.takeaway: The Neocloud Boom: State of AI Compute 2026 | Stephen Balaban. Maybe it's a long running agent workflow.
snippets.summary: Maybe it's a long running agent workflow. I think that that just means that everybody is able to process 10 times more tokens, and there's there's still the same fixed amount of compute in the world at any given point in time. Speaker 1 | 1:06:17 - 1:06:35 Well, I mean, a lot of the engineers at Lambda are already, you know, doing a fully agent driven workflow.
entities: The MAD Podcast with Matt Turck
